statement: The author-once-deploy-to-many-platforms capability (Markdown source -> Claude Code/Copilot/Cursor/Codex) is fully covered by mature open-source tools (ruler, rulesync), so agent-enterprise's distribution layer is reinvention, not novelty.

evidence.quote: Ruler centralizes AI coding assistant instructions in a single .ruler/ directory and automatically distributes them across 30+ agents (Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI...).
Generates configuration files... Supports rules, ignore files, MCP, commands, subagents, skills, hooks, and permissions across 30+ platforms.

falsifier: Show a concrete artefact transformation agent-enterprise performs that ruler/rulesync cannot — trust-label propagation across the include/@ref graph, capability-manifest validation, or per-target compiled approval gates — AND that bolting agent-enterprise's safety layer onto their output is materially harder than building distribution from scratch.
